perturbation.original_text: 6. REMUNERATION 6.1 The Company will pay the Executive a salary of £740,000 and a fixed benefits allowance of £30,000 per annum. The salary (and so far, as is reasonably possible) the benefits allowance will accrue from day to day and be payable in equal instalments in arrears on or around the 25th day of every month, less deductions for income tax and National Insurance contributions and shall be inclusive of any fees receivable by the Executive as a director of any Group Company. 6.2 The Executive's salary will be reviewed by the Compensation Committee every two years. There will be no salary review after notice to terminate this Agreement has been given by either party. The Company has no obligation to increase the Executive's salary following a review. 6.3 The Executive will be eligible to participate in any bonus or discretionary remuneration plan on such terms as the Compensation Committee may from time to time decide and always subject to the terms of the Executive Remuneration policy as approved by shareholders of WPP plc and to additional terms and conditions including the malus and clawback provisions of all relevant share or stock plans and as referred to in Schedule 2.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. REMUNERATION 6.1 The Company will pay the Executive a salary of £740,000 and a fixed benefits allowance of £30,000 per annum. The salary (and so far, as is reasonably possible) the benefits allowance will accrue from day to day and be payable in equal instalments in arrears on or around the 25th day of every month, less deductions for income tax and National Insurance contributions and shall be inclusive of any fees receivable by the Executive as a director of any Group Company. 6.2 The Executive's salary will be reviewed by the Compensation Committee at its discretion. The Company reserves the right to conduct more frequent reviews and adjust the Executive's salary based on performance and market conditions. There will be no salary review after notice to terminate this Agreement has been given by either party. The Company has no obligation to increase the Executive's salary following a review. 6.3 The Executive will be eligible to participate in any bonus or discretionary remuneration plan on such terms as the Compensation Committee may from time to time decide and always subject to the terms of the Executive Remuneration policy as approved by shareholders of WPP plc and to additional terms and conditions including the malus and clawback provisions of all relevant share or stock plans and as referred to in Schedule 2.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the salary review to be 'at its discretion,' it conflicts with the following sentence that the Executive's salary will be reviewed every two years. The amended section makes the frequency of reviews uncertain, creating an in-text contradiction. The original text specified a biennial review, while the modified version allows discretionary reviews, potentially undermining the expectation of a regular, predictable review cycle and the conditions of salary review are being changed, which are subject to performance and market conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2 The Executive's salary will be reviewed by the Compensation Committee every two years. There will be no salary review after notice to terminate this Agreement has been given by either party. The Company has no obligation to increase the Executive's salary following a review.
perturbation.justification: The original text states in 6.2 that "The Executive's salary will be reviewed by the Compensation Committee every two years." The changed text modifies this to "The Executive's salary will be reviewed by the Compensation Committee at its discretion." This creates a direct contradiction. The original text mandates a review every two years, while the changed text makes the review discretionary, thus removing the guaranteed biennial review. This alters the obligation of the company to conduct a review, leading to an in-text contradiction as it is a structural flaw, as per the definitions.

perturbation.original_text: 15. TERMINATION OF EMPLOYMENT 15.1 The Appointment may be terminated by either party giving the other at least 12 months' notice in writing. 15.2 The Company may in its sole and absolute discretion (whether or not any notice of termination has been given under sub clause 15.1) terminate this Agreement at any time and with immediate effect by giving notice in writing to the Executive that the Company is exercising its rights pursuant to this clause 15. If the Company elects to terminate the Executive's employment in this way, it will make, within 30 days, either the first instalment (of equal monthly instalments) of a, or an entire, payment in lieu of notice (Payment in Lieu) equal to the basic salary, benefit allowance and any benefits, as at the Termination Date, which the Executive would have been entitled to receive under this Agreement during the notice period referred to at sub clause 15.1 (or, if notice has already been given, during the remainder of the notice period), less all relevant deductions for income tax and National Insurance contributions.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. TERMINATION OF EMPLOYMENT 15.1 The Appointment may be terminated by either party giving the other at least 12 months' notice in writing. 15.2 The Company may, subject to mutual agreement with the Executive (whether or not any notice of termination has been given under sub clause 15.1) terminate this Agreement at any time and with immediate effect by giving notice in writing to the Executive that the Company is exercising its rights pursuant to this clause 15. If the Company elects to terminate the Executive's employment in this way, it will make, within 30 days, either the first instalment (of equal monthly instalments) of a, or an entire, payment in lieu of notice (Payment in Lieu) equal to the basic salary, benefit allowance and any benefits, as at the Termination Date, which the Executive would have been entitled to receive under this Agreement during the notice period referred to at sub clause 15.1 (or, if notice has already been given, during the remainder of the notice period), less all relevant deductions for income tax and National Insurance contributions.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'in its sole and absolute discretion' to 'subject to mutual agreement with the Executive', this creates an in-text contradiction with the original clause 15.2, which stated that the company can terminate the agreement in its absolute discretion. Now, it requires mutual agreement, making it unclear whether the company can unilaterally terminate the agreement.The initial phrase, 'in its sole and absolute discretion,' unequivocally grants the Company the power to terminate the agreement without needing the Executive's consent. However, the addition of 'subject to mutual agreement with the Executive' introduces a condition that directly opposes this unilateral right. This means the Company can only terminate the agreement if both parties agree, effectively negating the 'sole and absolute discretion' initially granted. The Company loses its right to terminate unilaterally, requiring Executive consent, creating an enforcement uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15.2 The Company may, subject to mutual agreement with the Executive (whether or not any notice of termination has been given under sub clause 15.1) terminate this Agreement at any time and with immediate effect by giving notice in writing to the Executive that the Company is exercising its rights pursuant to this clause 15. If the Company elects to terminate the Executive's employment in this way, it will make, within 30 days, either the first instalment (of equal monthly instalments) of a, or an entire, payment in lieu of notice (Payment in Lieu) equal to the basic salary, benefit allowance and any benefits, as at the Termination Date, which the Executive would have been entitled to receive under this Agreement during the notice period referred to at sub clause 15.1 (or, if notice has already been given, during the remainder of the notice period), less all relevant deductions for income tax and National Insurance contributions.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants the company 'sole and absolute discretion' to terminate the agreement, while the changed text requires 'mutual agreement with the Executive'. This directly contradicts the initial grant of unilateral power, creating a strong in-text contradiction as the Contradicted Text (which is the Changed Text) nullifies the 'sole and absolute discretion' originally afforded to the company in the original clause 15.2. The requirement for mutual agreement fundamentally alters the company's termination rights, creating uncertainty and enforceability issues.

perturbation.original_text: 11. HOLIDAYS 11.1 The Company's holiday year runs from 1 January to 31 December (the Holiday Year). The Executive is entitled to 25 days' paid holiday in addition to the usual public or bank holidays in England) in every Holiday Year, to be taken at times convenient to the Company. 11.2 No accrued but untaken holiday may be carried forward to the next holiday year and will lapse unless the Executive has been prevented from taking holiday due to sickness or statutory family leave to which he is or may be entitled further to Company policy from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. HOLIDAYS 11.1 The Company's holiday year runs from 1 January to 31 December (the Holiday Year). The Executive is entitled to 25 days' paid holiday in addition to the usual public or bank holidays in England) in every Holiday Year, to be taken at times convenient to the Executive. 11.2 No accrued but untaken holiday may be carried forward to the next holiday year and will lapse unless the Executive has been prevented from taking holiday due to sickness or statutory family leave to which he is or may be entitled further to Company policy from time to time.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'convenient to the Company' to 'convenient to the Executive' creates a direct contradiction in terms of holiday scheduling. The prior statement gives the company the authority, but know, gives the authority to the executive.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.1 The Company's holiday year runs from 1 January to 31 December (the Holiday Year). The Executive is entitled to 25 days' paid holiday in addition to the usual public or bank holidays in England) in every Holiday Year, to be taken at times convenient to the Executive.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants the company the power to determine when the Executive can take time off ('at times convenient to the Company'). The changed text gives the Executive this power instead ('at times convenient to the Executive'). However, the Contradicted Text also states the executive determines when to take time off ('to be taken at times convenient to the Executive.'). Therefore, the Change created a contradiction in terms of holiday scheduling, where both the company and executive determine time off, creating a direct conflict, making it an 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.
